title: One of the committees I sit on as the state senator for District 23 is Subcommittee No. 1 of the Senate Budget and Fiscal Review Committee. This subcommittee oversees education fun

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Proposition 49 established the After School Education and Safety (ASES) Program to create and grow local afterschool education and enrichment programs.",
    "The state provides $600 million annually in funding to ASES programs, distributed on a per-pupil basis to qualifying schools.",
    "ASES programs support over 4,000 elementary and middle schools and serve more than 400,000 students daily.",
    "The 21st Century Community Learning Centers (CCLC) program supports community learning centers providing academic enrichment opportunities for children during non-school hours, especially in high-poverty and low-performing schools.",
    "CCLCs receive five-year grant funding to provide K-12 students with academic opportunities and supportive services.",
    "The Expanded Learning Opportunities Grant (ELOG) funds are provided to local educational agencies to support supplemental instruction and social-emotional wellbeing due to student learning loss during the pandemic.",
    "The Expanded Learning Opportunities Program (ELOP) allocates funds based on the enrollment percentage of low-income, foster youth, and English learners to provide expanded learning opportunities.",
    "The shutdown of schools and remote learning during the pandemic significantly impacted children's education."
  ]
}
```
